title: Supplemental information for "Proximity proteomics reveals a co-evolved LRRK2-regulatory network linked to centrosomes"
author: Izzi, Francesca
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Step-by-step protocol for BioID-based interactome profiling of LRRK2 using HEK293 cells stably expressing a miniTurbo-LRRK2 fusion protein. The method allows for the identification of proximal interactors of LRRK2 under different cellular and pharmacological conditions, such as kinase inhibition (e.g., MLi-2, GZD-824) or Rab29 overexpression. 
 The protocol covers all experimental stages, including: 
 
 
 Cell culture and splitting of HEK293 miniTurbo-LRRK2 cells 
 
 
 Starvation and compound treatment combined with biotin labeling 
 
 
 Lysis and stringent protein extraction steps 
 
 
 StrepTactin-based immunoprecipitation of biotinylated proteins 
 
 
 Protein precipitation, in-solution digestion, and StageTip cleanup for LC-MS analysis 
 
 
 The procedure was optimized to capture transient and compartment-specific interactions of LRRK2. It is suitable both for stable and transient expression systems and can be adapted for BioID experiments with other bait proteins. 
 This protocol was used in support of the study  “Proximity proteomics reveals a co-evolved LRRK2-regulatory network linked to centrosomes”  (Eckert, Miglionico, et al.), which provides detailed insights into the co-evolved regulatory modules of LRRK2 relevant to Parkinson’s disease.
